purpose: Read the grouped trial balance rows for one grouping list + period. The balances[] row schema documented here is the source of truth for account/group/subgroup/fund keys.

url_notes.clientId: Axcess clientId (first int in the engagement URL)
url_notes.financialListId: grouping list id from GET /v1.0/financialList/{clientId} (the AUTHORITATIVE list inventory — not financialgrouptemplate)
url_notes.periodId: engagement period id; the CURRENT period id equals the URL's engagementId. Prior-period ids come from the engagement/period metadata (GET /v1.0/engagement/{clientId}) or the report-settings dialog captures.
url_notes.skip_take: PAGINATION IS MANDATORY for full reads: without ?skip&take the endpoint returns only the FIRST 50 accounts while the body's accountCount shows the true total — a silent partial read. Use scripts.groups.get_trialbalance_grouped_all (loops pages).

request.headers: ls:fp sentinel (KC localStorage, any kc-token tab) OR monkeypatch-captured WPM-leg headers from the engagement tab (bearer accepted by financialprep-api). XHR transport, never fetch.

shape.accountCount: int — TRUE total row count; compare against merged page length

account.id: int — CCH-internal account id (the referenceId used by FP annotations; NOT the account-number string)
account.accountNumber: string — e.g. '10010-201' (fund engagements suffix the fund)
account.group: string|null — financial GROUP name in this grouping list
account.financialSubGroup: string|null — the SUBGROUP key. ⚠ THIS is the populated field.
account.subGroup: ALWAYS NULL — legacy field name; reading it instead of financialSubGroup silently nulls every subgroup. Do not use.
account.fund: string|null — fund name/number on fund engagements; null on non-fund

current.sign_convention: ⚠ CREDITS POSITIVE (API-internal). This is NOT the TB import/export convention (debits positive) — FLIP SIGNS when building import files or comparing to exports. See modules/import-tb-format.md.

notes: Row array key is `balances` in current captures, but resolution should stay shape-agnostic (body | body.accounts | body.balances | body.trialBalance | first array prop) — scripts.groups.get_trialbalance_grouped_all already does this.
Period sub-objects beyond `current` appear when the engagement carries comparative periods; same balance-field shape.
accountCount is on the top-level body, not per page — use it as the loop terminator.

live_variant.warning: The endpoint ALSO serves a second row shape (scripts.tb_backup.normalize_tb_rows handles both). Do not assume the shape above — resolve keys defensively:

differences_vs_shape_above.account.name: replaces accountName
differences_vs_shape_above.account.financialSubGroup: an OBJECT {financialSubGroupId, index, name, order}, not a string
differences_vs_shape_above.account.group: an OBJECT {financialGroupId, id(GUID), index, name, classification, type{acctType,...}}, not a string
differences_vs_shape_above.account.fund: an OBJECT {id, index, name}, not a string
differences_vs_shape_above.account.subGroup: ABSENT entirely on this variant (the legacy always-null key does not even appear)
differences_vs_shape_above.periods.current: balances are COMMA-FORMATTED STRINGS with a trailing space ('1,843,250.00 ') under keys unadjusted / adjusted / final (plus dollarVar, percentVar, ibal*/obal*/oje*) — NOT numeric unadjustedBalance/adjustedBalance/finalBalance
differences_vs_shape_above.periods.prior: an ARRAY of prior-period objects (one per comparative period), NOT a single object — iterate it
differences_vs_shape_above.sign_convention: ⚠ this variant serves DEBITS-POSITIVE values — no flip vs the TB import/export convention, the OPPOSITE of the numeric variant's credits-positive rule above. Never hard-code either flip: spot-check one known account against a TB report before using balances.
differences_vs_shape_above.top_level: body also carries hasJe, id, periodEndDate, periods

live_variant.monitoring: tools/cch-drift probe fp_trialbalance_shape asserts the cross-variant invariants and fingerprints the current variant.

verify: Merged row count == accountCount; spot-check one known account's finalBalance against the TB report (remember the sign flip vs exports).
